top10.introHtml: Ranked by condition potential, reliability, and fun-per-dollar for a cheap 4-seat second car. 45 unique eligible listings across Cars.com, CarGurus, AutoTrader on 2026-07-21. 2-seat roadsters excluded (Miata, Z4, SLK, Boxster, T-bird 02–05, SC430, Allante, TT, etc.). Cars.com listings have clean title + no accidents filter applied.

picks.whyGood: S197 Mustang (2012) — the sweet spot of the nameplate. Proven 4.6 V8 (GT) or 4.0 V6, massive parts ecosystem. $11,459, 96,539 mi.
picks.whatToVerify: S197 (05–14): cam-phaser rattle cold-start on 4.6 3-valve (common on 05–10 GTs), spring-perch rust, top-latch wear. 8.8 rear axle clunk on takeoff, water staining on rear seats from top-seal failure.
picks.bottomLine: Best bang-for-buck muscle convertible in the pool — proven V8 power, easy DIY maintenance, huge community.

picks.whyGood: Toyota Camry Solara 2008 — arguably the most reliable 4-seat convertible under $15k. Camry underpinnings, simple soft top, low drama. 66,258 mi at $10,995.
picks.whatToVerify: Full top cycle (raise/lower twice), check headliner for water stains, rear-window seam, rocker rust. Strut mount noise, timing belt at 90k, top weatherstripping seals.
picks.bottomLine: Most reliable pick in the pool — Camry DNA means it'll run forever with basic maintenance.

picks.whyGood: S550-generation Mustang (2018) — modern platform, EcoBoost or V8, strong fun-per-dollar at $12,992 with 141,767 mi.
picks.whatToVerify: MT-82 shifter feel (notchy is normal, grinding is not). Rear-camera function. Convertible top latch sensors. Water staining on rear seat from top-seal failure. 8.8 axle clunk on takeoff.
picks.bottomLine: Modern Mustang at a bargain — EcoBoost or V8, bulletproof parts availability.

picks.whyGood: Buick Cascada 2017 — modern (2016+), smooth-riding 4-seat convertible with a turbo 1.6L and power soft top. Underrated and underpriced. 78,750 mi at $12,470.
picks.whatToVerify: Full top cycle, roof latch sensors, turbo intercooler hoses, transmission fluid. Check headliner for water stains, rocker-panel rust.
picks.bottomLine: Best modern pick at this price — worry-free daily driver that happens to drop the top.

picks.whyGood: Buick Cascada 2016 — modern (2016+), smooth-riding 4-seat convertible with a turbo 1.6L and power soft top. Underrated and underpriced. 71,211 mi at $11,994.
picks.whatToVerify: Full top cycle, roof latch sensors, turbo intercooler hoses, transmission fluid. Check headliner for water stains, rocker-panel rust.
picks.bottomLine: Best modern pick at this price — worry-free daily driver that happens to drop the top.

picks.whyGood: BMW 3-Series convertible 2009 — 4-seat soft-top, sharp handling, good power. $9,995 with 70,358 mi.
picks.whatToVerify: Full top cycle, check headliner for water stains, rocker rust. Oil leaks (valve cover, oil filter housing), soft-top hydraulic ram leak, cooling system (thermostat, hoses). SULEV emissions system if applicable.
picks.bottomLine: Driver's convertible at a fair price — budget for European maintenance costs.

picks.whyGood: Volvo C70 2011 — retractable hardtop, Euro styling, 4 true seats, above-average safety. $9,990 with 93,899 mi.
picks.whatToVerify: Full roof cycle, roof drains (front and rear — clog causes water intrusion), check headliner for stains, rocker rust. Timing belt service history (90k interval), AWD coupling if T5 AWD.
picks.bottomLine: Understated Euro hardtop — solid if roof drains are clear and timing belt is fresh.

picks.whyGood: Lexus IS 2010 Convertible — retractable hardtop, 4 proper seats, Lexus-grade reliability. Priced at $13,450 with 124,762 mi.
picks.whatToVerify: Full retractable roof cycle (watch for slow or jerky movement), trunk liner water stains, rear-camera operation. Check for TPMS faults, brake condition.
picks.bottomLine: Retractable hardtop with luxury feel and Toyota-level reliability — hard to beat at this price.

picks.whyGood: Chevy Camaro 1997 Z28 — real V8 muscle, 6-speed manual available, great soundtrack. 73,590 mi at $11,895.
picks.whatToVerify: Full top cycle, check headliner for water stains, rocker-panel rust. LT1/LS1 oil leaks, rear glass defroster, top frame alignment.
picks.bottomLine: Real V8 muscle at a bargain — if the top works and the body is clean, this is a steal.

picks.whyGood: Audi A3 Cabriolet 2016 — compact 4-seat soft-top, modern interior, 2.0T turbo. 83,000 mi at $11,999.
picks.whatToVerify: Full top cycle, check headliner for water stains. Timing chain, DSG fluid (S-tronic), water pump.
picks.bottomLine: Newest Audi in the pool — clean modern cabin and good value for the year.

manual10.introHtml: All 2 manual-transmission 4-seat convertibles found within 35 miles of Media, PA ($7k–$15k). Manual drop-tops are rare in this market.

picks.whyGood: Chevy Camaro 1997 Z28 — real V8 muscle, 6-speed manual available, great soundtrack. 73,590 mi at $11,895.
picks.whatToVerify: Full top cycle, check headliner for water stains, rocker-panel rust. LT1/LS1 oil leaks, rear glass defroster, top frame alignment. MANUAL-SPECIFIC: clutch engagement point (high bite = worn clutch), 2nd/3rd synchro slow-shift test, throw-out bearing chirp on startup, clutch hydraulics weeping.
picks.bottomLine: Real V8 muscle at a bargain — if the top works and the body is clean, this is a steal.

picks.whyGood: MINI Cooper 2007 Convertible — fun 4-seat soft-top, peppy engine, easy to park. 65,594 mi at $8,452.
picks.whatToVerify: Full top cycle, check headliner for water stains. MINI: timing chain tensioner (N14 engine especially), power steering fluid, top motor speed (slow = worn). MANUAL-SPECIFIC: clutch engagement point (high bite = worn clutch), 2nd/3rd synchro slow-shift test, throw-out bearing chirp on startup, clutch hydraulics weeping.
picks.bottomLine: Fun city car that drops the top — lots of character per dollar.

mustangs.introHtml: 5th-gen+ Ford Mustang convertibles (2005+) within budget and radius. 2 found. S197 (2005–2014) and S550 (2015+) only.

picks.whyGood: S197 Mustang (2012) — the sweet spot of the nameplate. Proven 4.6 V8 (GT) or 4.0 V6, massive parts ecosystem. $11,459, 96,539 mi.
picks.whatToVerify: S197 (05–14): cam-phaser rattle cold-start on 4.6 3-valve (common on 05–10 GTs), spring-perch rust, top-latch wear. 8.8 rear axle clunk on takeoff, water staining on rear seats from top-seal failure.
picks.bottomLine: Best bang-for-buck muscle convertible in the pool — proven V8 power, easy DIY maintenance, huge community.

picks.whyGood: S550-generation Mustang (2018) — modern platform, EcoBoost or V8, strong fun-per-dollar at $12,992 with 141,767 mi.
picks.whatToVerify: MT-82 shifter feel (notchy is normal, grinding is not). Rear-camera function. Convertible top latch sensors. Water staining on rear seat from top-seal failure. 8.8 axle clunk on takeoff.
picks.bottomLine: Modern Mustang at a bargain — EcoBoost or V8, bulletproof parts availability.
